data.title: Taysha Gene Therapies upgraded at Goldman Sachs on advancing Rett syndrome asset
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs has upgraded Taysha Gene Therapies (TSHA) to buy citing progress in its most advanced candidate.
  • The investment bank has a price target of $11 (~145% upside based on Dec. 3 close).
  • Analyst Salveen Richter said that the upgrade is due to the company moving Rett syndrome asset TSHA-102 into phase 3. Six-month interim data is expected in late 2026 or early 2027.
  • Taysha regained full rights to TSHA-102 from Astellas (OTCPK:ALPMF) in October. 
  • Richter is optimistic about results from the late stage trial, adding that it is  "differentiated from competitor [Neurogene's] (NGNE) registrational-stage Rett syndrome gene therapy based on clinical and safety data."
  • He estimates global peak sales of $3B based on pricing of $2.1M, which is similar to Novartis' (NVS) Zolgensma (onasemnogene abeparvovec).
  • Taysha is up ~11% in Thursday trading. It closed up ~11% on Dec. 3.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: TLRY, MRVL, QBTS, OKTA
data.description:
Recent analyst actions have spotlighted critical shifts in the market, with attention given to companies such as Tilray Brands (TLRY) and Marvell Technology (MRVL). Tilray has seen an improvement in its standing from a Strong Sell to Hold, reflecting more favorable market conditions. In parallel, Marvell Technology’s outlook has been upgraded, supported by optimistic revenue forecasts. Meanwhile, caution prevails with Okta (OKTA) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), both receiving downgrades primarily due to concerns over growth sustainability and valuation.

Upgrades

  • Tilray Brands, Inc. (TLRY): Upgrade from Strong Sell to Hold by Alan Brochstein, CFA. The upgrade is driven by the stock’s recent reverse-split and improved balance sheet, despite the ongoing concerns about the company’s strategy.

    “I am going from Strong Sell to Hold from an investment perspective… I like TLRY for a bounce, but my confidence in the company long-term is not so great. I am glad that they have fixed their balance sheet, though I fear more M&A.”

  • Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL): Upgrade to Buy by Tech Stock Pros. The upgrade highlights a promising revenue outlook for fiscal years 2027 and 2028, buoyed by strong performance in the company’s electro-optical business.

    “Marvell’s FY27 and FY28 outlook forces us to upgrade the stock back to a buy… We are confident in Marvell’s electro-optical positioning, which drives the bulk of its data center revenue.”

Downgrades

  • Okta, Inc. (OKTA): Downgrade to Hold by Amrita Roy. Slowing growth in revenue and diminished forward guidance prompt a more cautious stance, despite past earnings beats.

    “Although the management is optimistic about industry tailwinds pertaining to AI Security and the investments they are making towards their product innovation, it is not yet reflected in the forward guidance. As a result, investor sentiment continues to remain dampened in the stock.”

  • D-Wave Quantum, Inc. (QBTS): Downgrade to Sell by The Alpha Analyst. The downgrade reflects concerns about overvaluation and the speculative nature of the quantum computing sector, despite technological progress.

    “Given the valuation context, QBTS is best rated as a Sell today despite the progress. Markets are valuing QBTS as a market leader… This is a narrative-driven sector where investors might need to get used to high valuations.”

data.title: Shell, Petrobras increase stakes in two Brazil offshore oil projects after auction
data.description:
A consortium formed by Shell (SHEL) and Petrobras (PBR) said Thursday it increased its participation in Brazil's Atapu and Mero offshore pre-salt oil projects, after it was the only bidder in a public auction to acquire stakes in fields being divested by the Brazilian government.

Together with Petrobras (PBR), Shell (SHEL) said it deepened its stake in Atapu and Mero, acquiring 26.76% of Atapu Open Acreage (0.95% of the unit) and 20% of Mero Open Acreage (3.5% of the unit); with the acquisition, Shell increased its participating interest in the units from 16.663% to 16.917% in Atapu and from 19.3% to 20% in Mero.

Shell (SHEL) and Petrobras (PBR) agreed to pay a total of 8.79 billion reais (~$1.65 billion) for Atapu and Mero, according to UpstreamOnline.com; the two companies already partner in both fields, which are located in the offshore Santos Basin, one of the world's most productive offshore hydrocarbon regions.

data.title: The Fed should not be cutting interest rates in December - former CEA chair
data.description:
Former Council of Economic Advisers chair Jason Furman believes the Federal Reserve should not cut interest rates at its upcoming meeting, despite bond market odds suggesting a 90% probability of a rate reduction.

In an interview with CNBC, Furman, who now serves as a professor at Harvard Kennedy School, emphasized that inflation should remain the Fed’s primary concern, noting that while unemployment is nearly at target levels, inflation remains significantly above the Fed’s goal.

Furman acknowledged the economy’s uneven growth patterns but dismissed the idea that rate cuts would solve these disparities.

“We absolutely have a very uneven set of economic growth. It’s very concentrated in data centers. Housing’s being hit,” he said.

However, he maintained that the Federal Reserve cannot address these imbalances while inflation continues to run above trend.

The former Obama administration official pointed to several factors driving economic demand that reduce the need for monetary stimulus.

“We have a lot of other sources of demand, rising stock market, fiscal expansion, IT, data center expansion,” he said. These existing growth drivers, in his view, make additional monetary accommodation unnecessary and potentially counterproductive.

On the inflation front, Furman expressed concern about wage growth continuing to fuel price increases.

“Even in the weak ADP jobs numbers this week, the wage growth component of it was pretty strong,” he said.

He also highlighted the difficulty of reducing “non-housing service inflation” which has proven persistent as “wages go to prices; prices go to wages.”

Looking ahead, Furman warned about the risks of the Fed failing to achieve its stated 2% inflation target after four years of above-target inflation.

“People are really looking at them and wondering, do they mean it?”

Despite near-term concerns, Furman ended on an optimistic note about longer-term prospects. “I think we actually have real potential for growth in 2026. I’m not super worried about a recession right now.”

More on the U.S. economy

  • Initial jobless claims retreat to below 200K in Thanksgiving week
  • Market Voices: Bessent on tariff options; Pentagon's F/A-XX program
  • Bessent calls for change in how regional Fed presidents are chosen
  • Lutnick pushes back on tariff blame for negative ADP jobs print
  • ISM Services PMI rises more than expected in November

data.title: LendingClub named top 2026 fintech pick at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
LendingClub (LC) shares gained 3.2% in Thursday afternoon trading after J.P. Morgan named it a top fintech pick for investors next year.

"The company has been GAAP profitable since ‘22, and is transitioning to fair value accounting in January ‘26, which should level the playing field with other marketplace lenders, opening the door for faster origination growth and improved earnings optics (eliminating day-one “CECL tax”)," analyst Reginald Smith, who has an Overweight rating on LC, wrote in a note.

Smith upgraded LC in October after its Q3 earnings  showed encouraging signs that profit growth is poised to accelerate. Last month, Citizens upgraded the lending platform to Market Outperform as clarity around the company's growth drivers improved.

The SA Quant system rating ranks LC a Hold, compared with the average Wall Street analyst rating and the average SA analyst rating, both at Buy,

data.title: Mortgage rates down for second straight week
data.description:
Mortgage rates fell for the second consecutive week, according to the latest Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.19% as of December 4, down from 6.23% in the prior week and 6.69% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.44%, down from 5.51% a week ago and 5.96% in the year-ago week.

"Compared to this time last year, mortgage rates are half a percent lower, creating a more favorable environment for homebuyers and homeowners," said Freddie Mac's chief economist, Sam Khater.

data.title: Applied Materials sees bullish views at TD Cowen
data.description:
TD Cowen increased the price target on Applied Materials' (AMAT) stock to $315 from $260, while maintaining a Buy rating on the chip equipment maker.

"With -50% of the semis portfolio exposed to DRAM (non-China) and leading-edge Foundry, and given the potential for positive estimate revisions next year, AMAT is our best idea for 2026," said analysts led by Krish Sankar.

DRAM and NAND are two different types of memories.

The analysts noted that for non-China DRAM Wafer Fab Equipment, or WFE, (about 30% of  Applied Materials' semi systems), they model 17% growth in 2026 and see upside potential to +20%.

The analysts added that while they see a scenario where NAND WFE might outgrow DRAM WFE over the next two years — since its starting from a much lower base — they think the intermediate term setup favors DRAM WFE as NAND suppliers remain adamant about not adding any capacity. On the other hand, the analysts see a line of sight for multiple greenfield projects in DRAM — Samsung Electronics' (OTCPK:SSNLF) P4 mid-26; Micron Technology's (MU) Idaho; and SK hynix (OTCPK:HXSC.F) Yongin in 2027.

"For leading-edge Foundry, we model 15% growth in C26 that is second half weighted. It's early to pinpoint a growth number for C27, but checks indicate that it will be a stronger investment year (capacity is at full utilization today with multiple cleanroom projects coming online at TSM, Samsung/Tesla deal a tailwind as well)," said Sankar and his team.

Shares of  Applied Materials rose about 1% on Thursday.

data.title: PVH narrows full-year EPS guidance to $10.85-$11 while advancing brand innovation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: PVH Corp. (PVH) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Stefan Larsson detailed, "For the third quarter, we exceeded our guidance across reported revenue, operating profit and EPS, and we delivered constant currency revenues in line with our guidance. Total revenue for the company was $2.3 billion, down less than 1% in constant currency and in line with our expectations." Larsson reaffirmed the company’s focus on building Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger into premier lifestyle brands, emphasizing disciplined execution and a systematic approach through the PVH+ Plan.
  • Larsson reported double-digit growth in newly launched Calvin Klein women’s underwear styles, 20% growth in Icon Cotton Stretch underwear, and strong denim performance, noting viral global campaigns as key drivers. The launch of the Tokyo flagship and upcoming SoHo, New York, flagship store were highlighted as strategic retail expansion.
  • For Tommy Hilfiger, Larsson said, "This fall, Tommy opened its newest shop-in-shop concept at Galeries Lafayette in Paris, reflecting our multiyear elevation plan to evolve and invest in our shop-in-shops and stores. These investments bring a step change improvement in the consumer experience."
  • Larsson announced a leadership transition: "Patricia Gabriel...joined us last month as Chief Supply Chain Officer and Global Head of Operations...We announced that Zac Coughlin, our Chief Financial Officer, will be departing for a new opportunity outside of our industry...Melissa Stone will serve as our CFO."
  • CFO Zachary Coughlin stated, "We delivered our overall revenue plan and a sequential improvement in operating margin despite some choppiness in the quarter and an uneven global consumer backdrop. As a result, our EPS was better than expected."

Outlook

  • Coughlin provided, "We are reaffirming our full year constant currency revenue and operating margin guidance and narrowing our reported revenue and EPS guidance to the high end of the previous ranges."
  • Full-year EPS guidance was narrowed to $10.85 to $11, with operating margin outlook reaffirmed at approximately 8.5%. The company continues to expect tariffs to have a net negative impact of approximately $1.05 per share for the year.
  • Fourth quarter revenue is projected "to be up slightly to up low single digits on a reported basis and down slightly on a constant currency basis compared to the prior year." Q4 EPS is expected in the range of $3.20 to $3.35.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for the third quarter was $2.3 billion, with direct-to-consumer revenue down 1% in constant currency and wholesale revenue up 1%.
  • Gross margin for the third quarter was 56.3%, a decrease of 210 basis points compared to last year. Coughlin explained, "Progress on working through the Calvin Klein operational challenges continued, but our third quarter gross margin was lower than planned due to the unfavorable impact of timing and mix of the new higher tariffs."
  • EBIT was $202 million, and operating margin was 8.8%. Earnings per share was $2.83, including a negative impact of $0.37 related to tariffs.
  • Inventory was up 3% compared to last year, including a 2% increase due to tariffs.
  • The company completed an accelerated share repurchase program, bringing year-to-date total to 7.7 million shares.

Q&A

  • Robert Drbul, BTIG: Asked about regional dynamics. Larsson responded that Europe faced a muted consumer backdrop and operational challenges, while Americas growth was driven by e-commerce, and APAC exceeded plan, especially in China, Japan, and Australia.
  • Jay Sole, UBS: Inquired about marketing impact and inventory. Larsson described disciplined, category-focused marketing driving double-digit growth in key product areas, while Coughlin said, "We ended Q3 up 3% compared to last year, and that includes 2% impact of tariffs, so effectively flat to last year...we expect to have another strong free cash flow year this year."
  • Michael Binetti, Evercore: Asked about Calvin Klein product consolidation, margin recapture, and European weather. Larsson affirmed operational improvements were on track and discussed ongoing expansion in product franchises; Coughlin broke down margin headwinds and sequential improvement, noting, "We've gone from down 200 basis points of performance in first half to 50 in third quarter to now flat to last year in the fourth quarter."
  • Dana Telsey, TAG Advisors: Asked about Black Friday and holiday trends. Larsson observed strong Gen-Z engagement and an "on-plan" start for both Europe and North America.
  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about Calvin brand improvements and cost savings. Larsson outlined e-commerce consumer acquisition and improved loyalty programs; Coughlin confirmed over 200 basis points of SG&A improvement through Value Driver 5 initiatives.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about regional performance, margin headwinds, and operational progress, presenting a neutral tone with focus on execution and cost discipline.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, frequently referencing systematic improvement and proactive execution. During Q&A, management provided detailed breakdowns and reaffirmed guidance, reflecting steady confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management sentiment remained stable, emphasizing sequential improvements and targeted investments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 results were in line with guidance, with revenue and operating profit exceeding expectations as in Q2. Guidance was narrowed to the high end, compared to Q2’s reaffirmation.
  • Sequential improvement in gross margin headwinds was noted, with Q3 gross margin decline less pronounced than the prior quarter, attributed mainly to tariffs and product challenges.
  • SG&A expense as a percent of revenue improved 40 basis points versus last year, a continuation of efficiency gains seen in Q2.
  • The departure of CFO Zac Coughlin and interim appointment of Melissa Stone was a key management change not present in the prior quarter.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from macro uncertainty to operational execution and strategic marketing results.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted continued impact of tariffs in North America, with an expected net negative effect of approximately $1.05 per share for the year.
  • Transitory operational challenges from building the Calvin Klein global product capability were cited, with improvement plans on track for spring 2026.
  • Muted consumer activity in Europe and a shorter full price selling window for outerwear were noted as region-specific risks.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of margin improvements and the impact of global macro uncertainty.

Final Takeaway

PVH Corp. management emphasized that disciplined execution of the PVH+ Plan and focus on brand innovation led to third quarter results that met or exceeded guidance, despite ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and tariff impacts. The company reaffirmed and narrowed its full-year outlook to the high end, supported by strong product performance in key categories, continued efficiencies in SG&A, and progress in operational improvements. Strategic retail expansion and targeted marketing investments are expected to build momentum into 2026, while management remains vigilant about external risks and committed to systematic growth for Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Amazon unleashes its most powerful and efficient CPU: Graviton5
data.description:
Amazon Web Services (AMZN) has unleashed the Graviton5, which it said is its most powerful and efficient central processing unit, as the cloud and e-commerce giant continues to expand its chip offerings.

Although AWS continues to use chips from Nvidia (NVDA), Intel (INTC) and AMD (AMD) in its data centers, its leadership has always stressed the importance of offering its clientele an array of choices. This marks the third year in a row that more than half of the new CPU capacity added to AWS was powered by Graviton.

"Traditional approaches often force trade-offs, leaving you to choose between speed and efficiency," AWS said. "To address this need, today we are introducing Graviton5 processors—AWS's most advanced custom chip to date for a broad set of cloud workloads. Graviton5 delivers up to 25% better compute performance than the previous generation while maintaining leading energy efficiency, enabling you to run applications faster, reduce costs, and meet sustainability goals."

Some of the large-scale AWS customers utilizing Graviton chips include Adobe (ADBE), Airbnb (ABNB), SAP (SAP), Snowflake (SNOW), Synopsys (SNPS), Atlassian (TEAM), Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY), Pinterest (PINS), and Epic Games.

"Demanding workloads like real-time gaming, high-performance databases, big data analytics, application servers, and Electronic Design Automation can now scale up with faster data exchange between processing cores," AWS said.

Each Graviton5 core has access to 2.6x more L3 cache than the Graviton4. It also has 15% to 20% more network and storage bandwidth. It is built with 3-nanometer technology, which was also used on AWS' new Trainium3 chip, which was released earlier this week.

Graviton5-based M9g instances designed for general-purpose workloads are available in preview now. C9g instances for compute-intensive workloads and R9g instances for memory-intensive workloads are planned for 2026.

The announcements coincide with AWS's re:Invent 2025 conference being held this week in Las Vegas. AWS recently reached an annual revenue run rate of $132B.

data.title: Meta tests 50-day moving average following an early morning pop
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) saw an early morning surge that briefly pushed the mega-cap stock above its 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving averages. The initial pop lifted shares by more than 6% following a report that CEO Mark Zuckerberg plans to scale back metaverse projects by up to 30%.

This marks a notable rebound, as Meta had last closed above its 50-day moving average on October 29, before sliding lower in the wake of its most recent earnings report. Currently, the stock is hovering near its 50-day moving average, appearing to hold just below this key momentum level.

Within the broader Magnificent Seven group of mega-cap tech names, only Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL), Apple (AAPL), and Tesla (TSLA) are trading above their respective 50-day moving averages. In contrast, Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta Platforms (META) remain below this important technical threshold, reflecting ongoing pressure despite intermittent rebounds. 

Market participants will be closely watching whether META can sustain momentum above its 50-day line, a critical indicator for potential near-term recovery, while the rest of the group continues to navigate mixed technical signals amid a recent broader tech market rally that now has the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) just 2.4% away from its all-time trading high. 

Additional Data on META

  • 1-Month: +4.2%
  • 6-Month: -0.2% 
  • Year-to-Date: +13.6%
  • SA Quant Rating: 3.31
  • SA Analyst Rating: 4.05
  • Wall Street Rating: 4.61

Magnificent Seven Focused ETFs: (MAGS), (MAGX), (QQQU), and (QQQD).
